Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

what is the meaning of stars?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2007-08-10 · 8 answers
what is star? r they reflection of spiritual world or they r sun of other universe?
please clear my doubt.
user [19] · 2007-08-10
I heard they are mirrors of the sun.
user [24] · 2007-08-10
What we know in the west as the milky way is actually the Ganges as she flows from svarga into our realm. - Bhagavatam reference available upon request.
user [78] · 2007-08-10
Arent they just other planets? Inside the Devidhama?
user [124] · 2007-08-10
Stars sparkle because they all have hearts too.
user [19] · 2007-08-11
hearts? how is that?
user [24] · 2007-08-11
5th canto explains how the stars form the shape of a dolphin or something like that. im not expert.
user [12] · 2007-08-11
The scientists say the stars are trillions of miles away, etc. They get this because they say the earth is moving around the sun and they calculate triangulation from that movement. When you say the earth does not move around the sun it is a big problem (and you are a crackpot of course)
user [78] · 2007-08-12
<<<Stars sparkle because they all have hearts too.>>>

Wow! Cool ;)
